channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
item.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

item.title: Oil prices off highs after Trump abandons 20% protection fee on Strait of Hormuz traffic
item.description: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Bank earnings takeaways: What JPMorgan, BofA, Goldman, Citi and Wells Fargo said
item.description: The five banks reported strong revenue from equities trading.

item.title: U.S. trade official says 'very few' Nvidia H200 AI chips have been shipped to China
item.description: The remark is a sign that H200 shipments to China have restarted, potentially boosting Nvidia's sales even higher.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: IBM, Goldman Sachs, CleanSpark, HCA Healthcare & more
item.description: These are some of the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

item.title: Bank of America says investors are too bullish so forget about summer market upside
item.description: Strategist Michael Hartnett worries the sheer level of optimism is a contrarian sell signal, saying investors should reduce their exposure to stocks.

item.title: IBM stock craters 23% after issuing second-quarter earnings warning
item.description: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Barrett says 'the threat level is really high' in budget testimony
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan are testifying about the high court's budget request, which includes higher spending for security.

item.title: Chamath Palihapitiya says soaring AI token spend will hit companies' earnings
item.description: Palihapitiya is part of a growing chorus of investors and tech executives who are cautioning that the tokenmaxxing era is coming to an end.

item.title: ICE suspends vehicle stops for U.S. immigration enforcement, Reuters sources say
item.description: The policy shift came one day after an ICE officer killed a driver in the coastal Maine town of Biddeford, about 15 miles south of Portland.

item.title: United Airlines' new upsell: Keeping other travelers out of the middle seat
item.description: United Airlines will allow customers to pay more to keep the middle seat open on its Airbus A321XLRs

item.title: Social Security cost-of-living adjustment estimate for 2027 falls as inflation cools
item.description: New estimates show Social Security's annual cost-of-living adjustment for 2027 may be between 3.7% to 3.8%. Here's what to know.

item.title: Warren Buffett is accelerating his charitable donations with aim to give away Berkshire wealth by 2034
item.description: Warren Buffett is speeding up the pace of his annual donations of Berkshire Hathaway shares to four family foundations, giving them a total of almost $6 billion now.

item.title: E. Jean Carroll receives $5.6M from Trump in sex abuse, defamation case: Court filing
item.description: A federal judge recently ordered Trump to pay Carroll, rejecting his last-ditch efforts while noting that he "has been stalling this case for years."

item.title: Biotech's recovery isn't a 'head fake.' These 3 stocks could lead the next leg up
item.description: Biotech remains one of the market's more out-of-favor sectors despite improving industry fundamentals. Here are three stocks trading near multiyear lows.

item.title: Paramount still plans to close WBD merger by end of September despite lawsuit
item.description: Paramount is still planning for its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery to close by late September despite lawsuit challenge from state attorneys general.

item.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
item.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

item.title: Boeing records highest first-half deliveries since 2018
item.description: Boeing delivered 314 aircraft through the first half of the year, the highest number of first-half deliveries for the jet maker since 2018.

item.title: Sen. Tim Scott wants to hear from Warsh on data centers and artificial intelligence
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., will host Kevin Warsh before the Senate Banking Committee on Wednesday for his first time testifying as Fed chairman.

item.title: Google DeepMind chief Demis Hassabis calls for U.S. to spearhead AI standards body
item.description: Tech giant's AI boss said "urgent action" was needed as AI capabilities advanced.

item.title: Jim Cramer says don't bite on Apple sell call; buy his new chip favorite
item.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

item.title: Summer air travel can be ripe for delays. Here's how insurance can protect your trip
item.description: Delays, cancellations and other inconveniences can happen any time of the year, but getting insurance could be an especially good idea for your summer plans.

item.title: SpaceX alumni building remote-controlled construction equipment land $115 million fund round
item.description: TerraFirma on Tuesday said it raised $115 to support new hires and the building of a new factory and mission control center.

item.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
item.description: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

item.title: Consumers turn to buy now, pay later for essential expenses — with growing risks
item.description: Some consumers are using buy now, pay later for essential expenses like groceries, rent and utility bills. But more BNPL users have recently had a late payment.

item.title: Frontier Airlines to debut in-flight Wi-Fi in 2027 with SpaceX's Starlink
item.description: Frontier is signing with Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi, with service debuting as early as next year.

item.title: Mike Santoli: Why eye-popping earnings expectations have failed to lift the market for two months
item.description: The mantra heading into second-quarter results is that another display of stupendous growth of 24% over a year earlier makes equities appear quite reasonably priced.

item.title: Business declares war on Pentagon stock buyback, dividend restrictions moving in Senate
item.description: The provision was pushed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and was included in the National Defense Authorization Act on a bipartisan basis.

item.title: World Cup's biggest spenders show up late as semifinals drive host city travel boom
item.description: World Cup travel demand to U.S. host cities is spiking as the tournament reaches the semifinal stage.

item.title: Taiwan's second-largest chipmaker starts mass production in Singapore; Citi sees improving outlook
item.description: Taiwan’s UMC has kicked off mass production of silicon photonics wafers at its Singapore facility amid positive Citi forecast.
